tacticalRole: Flexible ranged unit used for map control, pressure and anti-air support.
strengths: High mobility with Blink
Useful against air and ground targets
Strong for poking and repositioning
weaknesses: Lower raw DPS compared to some dedicated damage units
Can be punished if Blink is unavailable
usageTip: Use Stalkers to poke, reposition and punish exposed units. Avoid taking direct fights without Blink or support.

tacticalRole: High DPS core infantry unit that scales extremely well with upgrades and support.
strengths: Excellent damage-to-cost ratio
Fast attack speed, especially with Stimpack
Versatile; hits both ground and air targets
weaknesses: Very fragile, highly vulnerable to splash damage
Requires active micro-management and Medivac support
Relies heavily on Combat Shields and Stimpack upgrades
usageTip: Always research Stimpack and Combat Shields early. Keep Marines paired with Medivacs and split them to minimize splash damage.

tacticalRole: Essential Terran worker unit. Gathers resources, constructs buildings, and repairs mechanical assets.
strengths: High HP (45) compared to other workers
Can repair mechanical units and structures
Can construct buildings anywhere on the map
usageTip: Enable auto-repair to quickly patch up mechanical units like Tanks, Thors, and Medivacs. Pull SCVs to repair Bunkers under pressure.

tacticalRole: Heavy frontline armored infantry. Excellent at absorbing damage and countering enemy armored ground forces.
strengths: High health pool (125 HP)
Bonus damage against armored units
Concussive Shells upgrade slows enemy units
weaknesses: Relatively low damage against light units
usageTip: Pair Marauders with Marines to absorb frontline damage and slow fleeing enemies, enabling Marines to clean them up.

tacticalRole: High-mobility early scout and harassment unit. Climbs cliffs and regenerates health out of combat.
strengths: Extremely fast movement speed
Can jump up and down cliffs freely
Out-of-combat health regeneration
weaknesses: Lacks scaled damage for mid-to-late game engagements
usageTip: Use KD8 Charges to disrupt enemy worker lines or to push back chasing units while scouting early.

tacticalRole: Elite psionic sniper and spellcaster. Destroys shields/energy with EMP and calls down tactical nuclear strikes.
strengths: Steady Targeting deals massive damage to biological units
EMP Round drains shields and spellcaster energy
Personal Cloaking for stealth recon
weaknesses: High gas cost and long production time
usageTip: Use EMP against Protoss armies to instantly delete shields, or against spellcasters like Vipers and Infestors to deplete their energy.

tacticalRole: Fast, lightly armored vehicle. Linear splash damage makes it ideal for clearing light ground swarms.
strengths: Incredible speed and mobility
Line-of-sight splash damage
Highly cost-effective (no gas cost)
weaknesses: Fragile when caught out of position
usageTip: Constantly patrol Hellions around map paths to deny Zerg creep tumors and intercept enemy run-bys.

tacticalRole: Frontline mechanical tank unit. Transformed from Hellion to act as a shield for fragile ranged units.
strengths: Large conical splash damage
Durable biological/mechanical armor tags
Healable by Medivacs
usageTip: Transform Hellions into Hellbats when facing mass Zergling/Baneling swarms or Zealot charges to create a strong protective wall.

combat.damage: 15 (+10 vs blindado) / 40 (+30 vs blindado) en asedio

tacticalRole: Terran tactical ground control. Devastating long-range area damage when deployed in Siege Mode.
strengths: Extreme range (13) in Siege Mode
Massive single-shot damage with area splash
Defines defensive zones
weaknesses: Vulnerable to air units or sudden close-range surrounds
usageTip: Position Siege Tanks on high ground or behind Bunkers/Hellbats to maximize their safety while they shell incoming ground forces.

tacticalRole: Skirmisher vehicle designed to lock onto and kite targets while moving.
strengths: High single-target damage with Lock-On
Fires while moving
Excellent early-game defense against single air/ground threats
usageTip: Use Lock-On on high-priority targets, then immediately retreat to kite them. Keep Cyclones in active movement.

tacticalRole: Burrowed mechanical trap. Fires devastating heat-seeking splash missiles.
strengths: Extremely cheap and easy to mass
Invisibility when burrowed
High burst area damage
usageTip: Burrow Widow Mines in mineral lines for worker defense, or place them along major map choke points to ambush incoming armies.

tacticalRole: Massive armored mechanical walker. High ground DPS and unmatched long-range anti-air firepower.
strengths: Massive health pool (400 HP)
Incredible anti-air range and damage with High Impact Payload
High ground attack damage
weaknesses: Highly vulnerable to magic spellcasters and swarms
usageTip: Use High Impact Payload against massive air units like Carriers, Tempests, or Brood Lords. Switch to Explosive Payload against Mutalisks.

tacticalRole: Transformable support ship. Dominates skies in fighter mode and lands to support ground forces.
strengths: Long anti-air attack range (9)
High bonus damage against armored flyers
Transformation adaptability
weaknesses: Weak damage output on ground in Assault Mode
usageTip: Use Vikings to target down massive high-threat units like Colossi or Brood Lords from maximum safety range.

tacticalRole: Dual-purpose tactical transport and healer. Provides vital healing and mobility to Terran bio forces.
strengths: Heals biological units continuously
Ignite Afterburners provides rapid bursts of speed
Enables powerful drop play
usageTip: Use Ignite Afterburners to escape dangerous situations or to zip past defensive lines for worker harass drops.

tacticalRole: Zone-control support ship. Fires splash anti-air missiles or sieges ground zones in Defender Mode.
strengths: Massive single-target ground damage in Defender Mode
Anti-air splash damage
Excellent for mineral line harassment
usageTip: Siege a Liberator over the enemy mineral line early in the game to completely shut down their economic collection.

tacticalRole: Robotic spellcaster and detector. Disables mechanical units and weakens armor with support missiles.
strengths: Interference Matrix disables enemy mechanical units/spellcasters
Anti-Armor Missile makes targets highly vulnerable
Permanent detection capability
usageTip: Disable key enemy massive threats like Siege Tanks or Colossi with Interference Matrix before launching a direct engagement.

tacticalRole: Cloaked tactical air-to-ground stealth harasser. Deals high single-target DPS.
strengths: Personal Cloaking allows stealth operations
High ground DPS output
Fast movement speed, especially with hyperflight rotors
usageTip: Harass enemy expansions and run away the moment detection arrives. Keep Banshees active to force defensive spending.

tacticalRole: Ultimate Terran capital ship. Spawns high-health threats, warps instantly, and targets with Yamato Cannon.
strengths: Tactical Jump warps anywhere on map
Yamato Cannon deals massive single-target damage (240)
Shoots while moving
usageTip: Use Yamato Cannon to instantly delete high-value static defenses or flyers, then use Tactical Jump to warp home to safety.

tacticalRole: Fast melee unit used for scouting, surrounds, countering ranged units, and harassment.
strengths: Unmatched speed on creep, especially with Metabolic Boost
Extremely cheap and cost-effective in mass numbers
Excellent for map control and counter-attacks
weaknesses: Very low individual health and armor
Struggles against chokepoints and defensive structures
usageTip: Use Zerglings to scout the map, surround enemy forces, and run into their bases when their army is out of position.

tacticalRole: Basic Zerg economic worker. Gathers resources and morphs itself into biological Zerg structures.
strengths: Regenerates health over time (passive Zerg biological trait)
Can burrow for safety if researched
Can escape threats by morphing into an extractor or spore crawler
weaknesses: Weakest direct attack of all worker units (5 damage)
usageTip: Burrow Drones if under early harassment to buy time for Queens or Zerglings to arrive and clean up the threat.

tacticalRole: Flying biological supply provider. Generates Creep and drops ground units when evolved.
strengths: Provides +8 supply, keeping the Swarm growing
Capable of flying over impassable terrain
Spreads creep in early stages to secure expansions
weaknesses: Very slow movement speed early on
Large target and easy source of supply blocks for enemies
usageTip: Always position early-game Overlords on safe high-ground pillars near enemy bases to monitor their build choices safely.

tacticalRole: Flying high-speed Zerg detector. Spawns Changelings and disables enemy production structures.
strengths: Detector of cloaked and burrowed threats
Spawn Changeling for free, low-risk scouting
Contaminate freezes enemy upgrades and production
usageTip: Cast Contaminate on enemy Robo Facilities or Tech Labs to stall key unit productions like Colossi or Siege Tanks.

tacticalRole: Base defense and macro anchor. Spawns Larvae, spreads Creep, and heals biological units with Transfuse.
strengths: Spawns Larvae to exponentially boost production
Transfuse instantly heals critical units/structures
Strong anti-air attack range (8)
weaknesses: Occupies supply-free defense roles but limits offensive maneuvers
usageTip: Always inject Larvae continuously. Spread Creep tumors aggressively to connect your bases and provide army speed boosts.

tacticalRole: Suicidal biological explosive. Destroys light infantry divisions and sweeps expansions.
strengths: Enormous splash damage against light targets
Highly effective against structures
Burrowed Banelings act as hidden traps
weaknesses: Fragile and vulnerable to targeted pre-emptive fire
usageTip: Always manual-target Banelings to hit light biological units, avoiding armored targets like Marauders that absorb the explosion easily.

tacticalRole: Armored biological frontline tank. High health, fast health regeneration, and moves while burrowed.
strengths: Excellent health pool (145 HP) for its cost
Extreme health regeneration while burrowed
Tunneling Claws allow movement while completely hidden
weaknesses: Low damage output against non-light targets
usageTip: Use Roaches as a frontline buffer. When health gets low, burrow them to regenerate instantly, then unburrow to resume the fight.

tacticalRole: Biles-throwing artillery unit. Breaks enemy defensive barriers and high ground sieges.
strengths: Corrosive Bile bypasses terrain and destroys Protoss Force Fields
No armored attribute tag, taking less damage from anti-armor attacks
Solid damage and range
weaknesses: Corrosive Bile requires high precision targeting
Vulnerable if caught with bile on cooldown
usageTip: Use Corrosive Bile to force enemy units to move, disrupting their positioning, or to break defensive Force Fields instantly.

tacticalRole: High-DPS ranged combat unit. Backbones the mid-game Zerg army against air and ground threats.
strengths: Very high single-target DPS
Attacks both air and ground with good range
Incredible speed boost on Creep with upgrades
weaknesses: Extremely fragile (90 HP) for its high cost
Relies heavily on creep and upgrades to be effective
usageTip: Always pair Hydralisks with a beefy frontline buffer like Roaches or Ultralisks to absorb incoming damage while they shred targets.

tacticalRole: Subterranean area siege unit. Shreds incoming ground waves with spikes fired while burrowed.
strengths: Devastating linear area-of-effect splash damage
Fires from absolute safety while burrowed
Outranges standard base defenses with upgrades
weaknesses: Cannot attack while unburrowed or moving
Vulnerable to air units and high-range sieges
usageTip: Burrow Lurkers at critical choke points or ramp entrances to deny enemy ground armies any access to your bases.

tacticalRole: Subterranean spellcaster and force multiplier. Traps armies and hijacks massive units.
strengths: Fungal Growth immobilizes and damages multiple units
Neural Parasite takes control of massive enemy units
Can cast all spells while completely burrowed
weaknesses: Extremely high gas cost and fragile health
usageTip: Use Fungal Growth to freeze high-mobility units in place, then follow up with Banelings or Corrosive Biles to sweep them.

tacticalRole: Indirect siege weapon. Launches waves of free, flying explosive Locusts to wear down defenses.
strengths: Generates free flying units (Locusts) to deal safe damage
Can bypass terrain and cliff defenses
Forces enemy to play defensively
weaknesses: Completely defenseless when Locusts are on cooldown
usageTip: Launch Locusts from a safe distance, then immediately retreat and burrow your Swarm Hosts during the long cooldown period.

tacticalRole: High-speed flying harasser. Bounces attacks to clean up worker lines and force splits.
strengths: Unmatched flight speed and acceleration
Glaive Wurm bounces damage across three targets
Extremely fast passive tissue regeneration
weaknesses: Fragile (120 HP) and expensive in gas
Highly vulnerable to area-of-effect anti-air defenses
usageTip: Never engage direct anti-air armies head-on. Constantly fly around the map edges to snipe expansions and exposed workers.

tacticalRole: Heavy anti-air fighter. Counters enemy capital flyers and melts structures with Caustic Spray.
strengths: High armor and health pool
Bonus damage against massive air units
Caustic Spray melts static defenses and expansions quickly
usageTip: After clearing the skies, use Caustic Spray on enemy Nexuses or Command Centers to quickly delete their bases.

tacticalRole: Advanced aerial tactical spellcaster. Disables ranged units and abducts high-value targets.
strengths: Abduct pulls high-value targets directly into your army
Blinding Cloud reduces enemy ranged attacks to melee range
Parasitic Bomb decimates air swarms
weaknesses: Very fragile and high-priority sniper target
usageTip: Use Abduct to pull expensive units like Siege Tanks or Colossi out of their defensive lines and directly into your waiting army.

tacticalRole: Late-game massive biological battering ram. Absorbs damage and cleaves ground armies.
strengths: Massive health pool (500 HP) and high base armor
Chitinous Plating upgrade makes them highly tanky
Cleave attack damages multiple ground units
weaknesses: Huge collision size; easily blocked by terrain/chokes
Vulnerable to single-target anti-armor high DPS
usageTip: Support Ultralisks with Infestors (Fungal Growth) or Vipers (Blinding Cloud) to prevent enemies from kiting them.

tacticalRole: Massive Zerg biological capital ship. Spawns endless Broodlings to siege ground armies.
strengths: Enormous siege attack range (10)
Spawns Broodlings that physically block and distract ground units
Extremely high threat level
usageTip: Always escort Brood Lords with a strong screen of Corruptors or Hydralisks to protect them from high-range anti-air threats.

tacticalRole: Protoss robotic worker. Gathers resources and warps in structures by deploying placement beacons.
strengths: Shields regenerate automatically out of combat
Only needs to deploy a warp beacon, leaving it free to perform other tasks immediately
Can scout early with high survivability due to shield health
weaknesses: Fragile and susceptible to worker sweeps
usageTip: Warp in multiple pylons or buildings sequentially to minimize idle time. Never make Probes wait while warping.

tacticalRole: Frontline melee shock trooper. Charging upgrade grants high-speed engagement and heavy tanking.
strengths: Excellent health-to-shield ratio (150 total)
Charge upgrade closes gaps instantly and strikes targets
Highly cost-effective mineral dump in late-game
usageTip: Always research Charge at the Twilight Council early. Use Zealots as a frontline shield for fragile ranged units like High Templar or Stalkers.

tacticalRole: Robotic support spellcaster. Creates physical barriers, shields allies, and projects illusions.
strengths: Force Fields cut armies in half or block ramp entrances
Guardian Shield reduces incoming ranged damage by 2
Hallucination enables risk-free scouting
usageTip: Use Force Fields to trap half of the enemy army, converting a risky engagement into a highly favorable, isolated battle.

tacticalRole: Early game ranged harasser. Projects a mobile shade to teleport into mineral lines.
strengths: Psionic Transfer allows teleporting past wall-ins
Bonus damage against light units
High health and shields for early game
weaknesses: Poor scaling in late-game direct fights
usageTip: Use Psionic Transfer to shade into Zerg or Terran mineral lines. Cancel the shade if defenses are ready, or let it complete to instantly execute workers.

tacticalRole: Premier psionic spellcaster. Wreaks havoc with Psi Storms and disables spellcasters with Feedback.
strengths: Psi Storm deals massive damage over time in a wide area
Feedback drains enemy energy and inflicts equivalent damage
Morphs into an Archon when energy is depleted
usageTip: Keep High Templar on a separate hotkey behind your army to prevent them from walking into the front line. Cast Feedback on incoming Vipers or Medivacs immediately.

tacticalRole: Invisible elite assassin. Infiltrates bases silently and blinks with Shadow Stride.
strengths: Permanently invisible without detection
Incredible single-target physical damage (45)
Can merge into an Archon
usageTip: Sneak Dark Templar into multiple expansions simultaneously. If detection arrives, quickly merge them into Archons to preserve your investment.

tacticalRole: Psionic energy powerhouse. High splash damage against biological threats and massive shields.
strengths: Massive shields (350)
Splash damage against biological units
No biological/mechanical tags, neutralizing anti-armor damage bonuses
weaknesses: Extremely low health (10 HP), making them vulnerable to EMP
usageTip: Place Archons in the front line alongside Zealots. Their splash damage shreds biological units, and their massive size blocks enemy paths.

tacticalRole: Invisible robotic spy. Provides permanent area detection and recon.
strengths: Permanent cloaking
Mobile detector of cloaked/burrowed hazards
Surveillance Mode expands vision range by 25%
weaknesses: Can be targeted if caught by detector sweeps
usageTip: Place Observers in Surveillance Mode over key map pathways and outside enemy bases to trace their army movements without risk.

tacticalRole: Tactical dimensional transport. Projects a mobile power field to warp units directly onto the front lines.
strengths: Phasing Mode creates a warp-in field anywhere
Enables micro-saves by picking up units from high ranges
Increases army mobility significantly
usageTip: Fly a Warp Prism into the back of an enemy base, switch to Phasing Mode, and warp in a squad of Zealots or Dark Templar to devastate their economy.

tacticalRole: Heavy robotic siege breaker. Barrier shield absorbs heavy bursts of anti-armor fire.
strengths: High single-target damage against armored units
Barrier shield absorbs up to 100 damage
Incredibly tanky frontline fighter
usageTip: Target Immortals against armored threats like Roaches or Stalkers. Use a Warp Prism to lift them out of danger when their Barrier shield is down.

tacticalRole: Robotic walker. Sweeps linear thermal lances to incinerate light ground armies.
strengths: Long-range (up to 9 with upgrade) area sweep
Bypasses terrain and scales cliffs freely
Utterly vaporizes light infantry/swarms
weaknesses: Targetable by both ground and air attacks
Fragile for its high resource cost
usageTip: Always research Extended Thermal Lance early. Keep the Colossus far behind your stalker/zealot army to maximize its safety while it sweeps targets.

tacticalRole: Tactical robotic shock weapon. Launches Purification Novas to delete compact ground units.
strengths: Purification Nova deals massive, instant lethal area damage
Forces extreme kiting/splitting from enemies
Excellent zone denial
weaknesses: Completely vulnerable during the long cooldown period (21s)
usageTip: Launch Purification Novas into choked areas or compact armies, forcing them to scatter and disrupt their positioning.

tacticalRole: Ultra-fast aerial interceptor. Attacks while moving and lifts ground units with Graviton Beam.
strengths: Fires automatically while moving
Graviton Beam lifts high-value ground units out of combat
Exceptional speed and acceleration
weaknesses: Low single-target damage against armored units
Cannot attack ground targets without using energy
usageTip: Use Phoenixes to clear out light flyers like Mutalisks. In ground fights, lift key units like Siege Tanks or Lurkers to disable them.

tacticalRole: Heavy attack ship. Prism Beam scales damage continuously against armored capital units.
strengths: Prismatic Alignment deals massive damage to armored units
Solid health and speed
Strong in defensive configurations
weaknesses: Vulnerable to mass swarms of cheap units
usageTip: Activate Prismatic Alignment when engaging armored targets like Corruptors or Stalkers to melt their health pools instantly.

tacticalRole: Aerial tactical harasser and scout. Deploys stasis traps and activates Pulsar Beam.
strengths: Pulsar Beam deletes workers in seconds
Revelation reveals cloaked units and map fog
Stasis Ward traps incoming armies
usageTip: Place Stasis Wards on ramps or chokepoints to freeze early-game pressure in place. Use Revelation to monitor enemy expansions.

tacticalRole: Long-range capital aerial sniper. Fires high-impact shots from extreme ranges.
strengths: Extreme attack range (14 vs ground, 10 vs air)
Excellent anti-capital sniper capability
Keeps Protoss safe from static defense zones
weaknesses: Weak against massive swarms of light units
usageTip: Use Tempests to pick off dangerous siege units like Siege Tanks or Liberators from outside their engagement range.

tacticalRole: Ultimate Protoss capital ship. Launches swarms of Interceptors to overwhelm targets.
strengths: Interceptors automatically distract and block single-target attacks
Huge health and shield pools
Incredible offensive presence
weaknesses: Extremely expensive and long build time
Vulnerable if all Interceptors are cleared
usageTip: Keep Carriers in a tight group to maximize their Interceptor cloud size, overwhelming anti-air targeting systems.

tacticalRole: Ultimate flagship support unit. Cloaks the entire army, warps forces, and slows time.
strengths: Permanently cloaks all nearby friendly units
Time Warp slows down enemy unit movement and attacks
Strategic Recall warps your entire army back to base safety
weaknesses: Only one Mothership can be built at a time
Massive priority target for all anti-air units
usageTip: Position the Mothership directly above your high-value army. Use Time Warp on enemy lines and be ready to trigger Strategic Recall if the fight turns bad.
